mission.title: etcd: auth token invalid after watch reconnects
mission.description: auth token invalid after watch reconnects. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current etcd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your etcd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd
helm list -n etcd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working etcd installation.

steps.title: Review etcd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant etcd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd
kubectl get configmap -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=etcd
```
# Reproduce Procedure

1. prepare etcd cluster with 3 nodes and respective etcdctl

```

etcd Version: 3.4.9
Git SHA: Not provided (use ./build instead of go build)
Go Version: go1.14.2
Go OS/Arch: linux/amd64
```

though I don't think configure

steps.title: Apply the fix for auth token invalid after watch reconnects
steps.description:
Sorry for late reply, I was on sick leave during last a few days.

In my first attempt, I was going to implement refresh on the grpc mechanism, PerRPCCredentials.GetRequestMetadata, until I encountered a grpc issue https://github.com/grpc/grpc-go/issues/3749, which was  fixed in
```yaml
/go/src/github.com/coreos/etcd # etcd --version
etcd Version: 3.4.9
Git SHA: Not provided (use ./build instead of go build)
Go Version: go1.14.2
Go OS/Arch: linux/amd64
```

steps.title: Upgrade etcd to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade etcd:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade etcd etcd/etcd --namespace etcd
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n etcd
helm list -n etcd
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd
kubectl get events -n etcd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "auth token invalid after watch reconnects" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Sorry for late reply, I was on sick leave during last a few days.
codeSnippets:
/go/src/github.com/coreos/etcd # etcd --version
etcd Version: 3.4.9
Git SHA: Not provided (use ./build instead of go build)
Go Version: go1.14.2
Go OS/Arch: linux/amd64

name: etcd2
data-dir: /data/zz_6129484611666145821
listen-peer-urls: http://0.0.0.0:23800
listen-client-urls: http://0.0.0.0:23790
initial-advertise-peer-urls: http://10.213.20.39:23800
advertise-client-urls: http://10.213.20.39:23790
initial-cluster: etcd0=http://10.213.20.40:23800,etcd1=http://10.213.20.38:23800,etcd2=http://10.213.20.39:23800
initial-cluster-token: zz
initial-cluster-state: new
auto-compaction-retention: "1"
quota-backend-bytes: -1

etcdctl user add root
# then type root password, mine is 'root'
etcdctl --user root:root auth enable

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with etcd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
